content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 23, 2014

David A. Prentice, County Counsel
Office of the County Counsel
P O Box 1428
Weaverville, CA 96093-1428

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-14-111

Dear Mr. Prentice:

This letter responds to your request for advice dated June 20, 2014, regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). We note your request asks about application of the doctrine of incompatible offices, which is not under the Commission's purview. The Commission can only advise on the provisions of the Act (Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014).¹ You may wish to contact the Office of the Attorney General, Post Office Box 944255, Sacramento, CA 95814 with respect to your questions.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Zackery P. Morazzini
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

JWW:jgl

¹ We have enclosed a copy of California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B), which explains the procedures for seeking assistance from the Fair Political Practices Commission.

sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked about the application of the doctrine of incompatible offices under the Political Reform Act's conflict of interest provisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the doctrine of incompatible offices is not within the Commission's purview; the FPPC can only advise on Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014, and the requester was directed to contact the Office of the Attorney General.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter (File No. W-14-111W indicates withdrawal/declination). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The legal question and FPPC's conclusion were synthesized from the letter's body text explaining the scope limitation.

QUESTION: The requester asked about the application of the doctrine of incompatible offices under the Political Reform Act's conflict of interest provisions.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the doctrine of incompatible offices is not within the Commission's purview; the FPPC can only advise on Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014, and the requester was directed to contact the Office of the Attorney General.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined an advice request from the County Counsel regarding the incompatible offices doctrine, stating it falls outside the Commission's jurisdiction and recommending the requester contact the Attorney General instead.
